title: Relations between different definitions of the quantum Wasserstein distance for qubits
abstract: The quantum Wasserstein distances defined by Golse, Mouhot, Paul, and Caglioti and by De Palma and Trevisan coincide for qubits when a single operator appears in the cost function. As a consequence, the self-distance equals the Wigner-Yanase skew information in this case.
summary: This paper proves that two different definitions of quantum Wasserstein distance are identical for qubits when using a single operator in the cost function. It further demonstrates that this equivalence allows the ground state energy of certain spin chains to be expressed directly via the Wigner-Yanase skew information.
main_problem: The paper investigates the relationship between two different definitions of the quantum Wasserstein distance for qubits: the DGMPC definition and the DPT definition.
main_result: The authors prove that for qubits, the DPT and DGMPC distances are equivalent when the cost function involves a single operator, and they show that the self-distance in this case equals the Wigner-Yanase skew information.
method: The study uses quantum optimal transport theory, involving optimization over bipartite density matrices (couplings) with fixed marginals, and employs unitary transformations to simplify the operators into real matrices.
model_or_system: The study focuses on two-level quantum systems (qubits) and considers the context of ferromagnetic spin chains.
key_observables: Quantum Wasserstein distances (D_DPT and D_DGMPC), Wigner-Yanase skew information, Quantum Fisher Information, and ground state energy of spin chains.
important_parameters: The case where the cost function contains a single operator (N=1).
main_assumptions: The proven equivalence is specifically restricted to qubits and the case of a single operator in the cost function.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the two Wasserstein distance frameworks, introduces a lemma for unitary rotation to real matrices, provides a proof for the equivalence theorem for qubits, and concludes by relating the results to the ground state energy of spin chains.
why_interesting: This is relevant for researchers in quantum information and many-body dynamics as it establishes a fundamental link between quantum optimal transport metrics and information-theoretic quantities like the Wigner-Yanase skew information.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
